name: Kahlua Mocha Mousse
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 ounces Bittersweet chocolate, cut into small pieces
1 1/2 ounces Unsweetened chocolate, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
For the Mousse:
1. In the top of a double boiler set over barely simmering water, melt the chocolates with the coffee and the Kahlua, stirring occasionally.
2. In a bowl with an electric mixer, beat the egg yolks with 3 tablespoons of the sugar until the mixture is thick and pale. Stir in the chocolate mixture.
3. In another bowl, beat the egg whites with a pinch of salt until they are frothy. Add the cream of tartar and beat the whites until they hold soft peaks.
4. Gradually beat in the remaining 1 tablespoon of sugar into the whites, then beat the whites until they just hold stiff peaks.
5. Stir 1/4 of the whites into the chocolate mixture, then gently fold in the remaining whites and the whipped cream.
6. Pour the mousse into a 1 quart shallow dish and chill, covered, for at least two hours (this may be made up to one day in advance).
7. Before serving, arrange some of the chocolate leaves on the mousse and serve the remaining leaves separately.
Chocolate Leaves:
1. In the top of a double boiler set over barely simmering water, melt the chocolate.
2. With a spoon, coat the back (non-shiny) side of each leaf with the melted chocolate, being careful not to let the chocolate drip onto the shiny side.
3. Place the leaves, chocolate side up, on a jelly roll pan lined with wax paper and prop the edges of the leaves with pieces of foil or paper towel to allow the edges to curl.
4. Chill the leaves for 20 minutes, or until the chocolate has hardened.
5. Working quickly, peel off the lemon leaves (if the chocolate gets too soft, chill the leaves for an additional 5 minutes or until hardened).
6. Keep the leaves chilled until just before serving.
